name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BY PASS 41

surface_od_m.qualifier: as written (Ground level 85.30 m O D)

units: metres (m), typed Wimpey Geotech site-investigation log; depth_m is BASE of interval below ground level; Reduced Level (O D Level m O D) column used only to cross-check

annotations: # Description based on Driller's log (per Remarks, sheet 1)
# Description based on Driller's log (per Remarks, sheet 1)

intervals.verbatim: Stiff mottled dark brown and grey sandy silty CLAY with some fine to medium subangular and subrounded gravel
Firm dark greyish brown laminated silty CLAY with some layers of brown fine sand
Loose occasionally medium dense light brown becoming brown silty fine becoming fine to medium becoming medium to coarse SAND. Coal dust throughout
annotations: Remarks: water was added to assist boring from 3.35m to 7.40m and may have masked the occurrence of ground-water

intervals.verbatim: Firm to stiff becoming stiff to very stiff dark brown sandy becoming very sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel (Glacial Till)
annotations: Remarks: borehole advanced by chiselling 8.85m-9.20m (half hr) and 10.80m-11.15m (half hr)
U(100) 116 blows at 9.20m and 130 blows at 10.50m — very dense

intervals.verbatim: Firm dark brown sandy silty CLAY and fine to coarse subangular and subrounded GRAVEL and COBBLES
annotations: Remarks: borehole advanced by chiselling 13.35m-13.90m (1 hr)
S>50 for 125mm at 13.90m (SPT refusal on cobbles)
OD level 71.40 marked at 13.90m base

intervals.verbatim: Yellowish brown coarse SAND and fine to coarse subangular GRAVEL and COBBLES
annotations: Base of superficial deposits; cable-percussion terminated and rockhead reached at 14.00m (casing dry at 14.00m)

Assigned to 13.90-14.00m per the three basal OD marks (71.80/71.40/71.30 at 13.50/13.90/14.00); the description text is drafted below the 14.00m boundary line for space, hence the interpretive placement

intervals.verbatim: Orange becoming pale yellow occasionally white fine to medium grained highly discoloured slightly and slightly to moderately weathered thinly bedded faintly planar laminated micaceous SANDSTONE - strong, occasionally moderately strong. Closely and very closely spaced subhorizontal to 10 degree bedding fractures commonly with coal dust on fracture surfaces. Occasional highly fractured bands and very occasional weak, disintegrated bands. Occasional dark ironstained subvertical fractures
annotations: Rotary core drilling from 14.00m; 15.40m and 18.20m are core-run boundaries, not lithological
coal dust on fracture surfaces

intervals.verbatim: White locally highly discoloured orange medium grained moderately weathered thinly bedded SANDSTONE - moderately strong and strong. Many thin (less than 5mm) wavy laminae and lenses of coal. Very closely spaced 10 degree bedding fractures. Core predominantly highly fractured below 21.20m
annotations: thin (<5mm) wavy coal laminae and lenses
core predominantly highly fractured below 21.20m

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained moderately becoming moderately to highly weathered thinly planar laminated sandy SILTSTONE - moderately strong. Bed of white fine grained thinly laminated sandstone with siltstone laminae from 23.05m to 23.70m. Core predominantly highly fractured with much silty clay below 24.25m
annotations: contains a bed of white fine-grained sandstone with siltstone laminae 23.05m-23.70m
Remarks: ground-water encountered at 24.25m (GWe mark in water column)
much silty clay below 24.25m

intervals.verbatim: Interbedded white fine grained slightly weathered thinly laminated silty micaceous SANDSTONE - strong and dark grey fine grained slightly and slightly to moderately weathered thinly laminated sandy SILTSTONE - moderately strong. Medium and closely spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures. Some 45 degree fractures within siltstone beds. Some highly fractured bands of siltstone. Highly weathered highly fractured sandstone with much brown silty clay from 28.40m to 28.60m
annotations: Interbedded sandstone AND siltstone unit; classed sandstone (arenaceous dominant) — siltstone interbeds noted
some 45 degree fractures within siltstone beds
highly weathered highly fractured sandstone with much brown silty clay 28.40m-28.60m
27.00m and 28.40m are core-run boundaries

intervals.verbatim: White fine and fine to medium grained slightly weathered bedded micaceous SANDSTONE - strong occasionally moderately strong to strong. Close to medium becoming closely and very closely spaced subhorizontal to 10 degree bedding fractures. Occasional 60 degree to subvertical fractures. Discoloured brownish grey highly becoming moderately weathered moderately weak becoming moderately strong with many thin coal laminae from 30.25m to 30.95m. Many very closely and extremely closely spaced wavy coal laminae up to 3mm thick below 32.65m
annotations: many thin coal laminae 30.25m-30.95m
many wavy coal laminae up to 3mm thick below 32.65m
occasional 60 degree to subvertical fractures

intervals.verbatim: Grey slightly and moderately weathered CONGLOMERATE - strong. Coarse gravel and cobble sized elongate subrounded dark grey siltstone clasts are set in pale grey fine grained sandstone matrix. Very closely occasionally closely spaced irregular fractures. Dark grey predominantly highly fractured siltstone from 33.35m to 33.65m
annotations: Logged as CONGLOMERATE (rudaceous) — no conglomerate class available, mapped to sandstone (pale grey fine-grained sandstone matrix with dark grey siltstone clasts)
top drawn at 33.25m vs white-sandstone base at 33.20m — 0.05m sliver as drafted, treated as coincident boundary
dark grey highly fractured siltstone 33.35m-33.65m

intervals.verbatim: Greyish white fine grained slightly weathered faintly thinly laminated SANDSTONE - strong. Many thin (up to 3mm) coal laminae. Very closely spaced 5 degree to 10 degree bedding fractures usually along coal laminae
annotations: many thin (up to 3mm) coal laminae; bedding fractures usually along coal laminae

intervals.verbatim: Black becoming dark grey fine grained highly weathered highly fractured silty MUDSTONE - weak
annotations: thin weak mudstone band within the Coal Measures sandstone sequence

intervals.verbatim: Pale greyish white fine grained moderately weathered faintly thinly laminated SANDSTONE - strong. Many thin rippled siltstone laminae and occasional siltstone bands. Some highly weathered siltstone bands above 38.00m. Very closely and closely spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures. Common subvertical often ironstained fractures below 38.50m. Many highly fractured bands
annotations: thin rippled siltstone laminae and occasional siltstone bands (some highly weathered above 38.00m)
common subvertical often ironstained fractures below 38.50m
END OF BOREHOLE at 39.30m

notes: Modern typed rotary-cored site-investigation log (Wimpey Geotech, Fig. 45), sheets 1-4 of 4. Record of BOREHOLE 41. Boring method: Cable Percussion to 14.00m, Rotary Core Drilling to 39.30m; equipment Pilcon Wayfarer / Hands England 36/80 (Tractor Mounted) / 412 Core Barrel / Air Flush. Ground level 85.30 m O D; commenced 25/4/90 (cable percussion), rotary from 30/4/90; originator MRW 25/4/90, checked/approved AMcL 22/6/90. Local grid location 5748.6E/2235.5N on sheet; BNG 425742,542244. Reduced-Level cross-check: every marked O D level equals 85.30 minus depth exactly (e.g. 7.40->77.90, 22.50->62.80, 39.30->46.00) — no clerk arithmetic slips. Drift 0-14.00m (topsoil, clays, sand, glacial till, basal clay+gravel+cobbles, thin yellowish basal sand+gravel+cobbles); rockhead / Coal Measures sandstone at 14.00m. INTERPRETIVE CALL at the base of drift: three near-coincident O D marks (71.80/71.40/71.30 at 13.50/13.90/14.00m) resolve into three thin beds; the 'Yellowish brown coarse SAND...' description is drafted below the 14.00m line but assigned to 13.90-14.00m to honour the O D marks and the cable-percussion terminus (interval 9 flagged 'probable'). Bedrock is Coal Measures: sandstones with abundant coal dust / coal laminae and lenses, sandy siltstone, an interbedded sandstone-siltstone unit, a conglomerate (mapped to sandstone class), and a thin weak silty mudstone band (35.95-36.30m). No NAMED or worked coal seam is present (only coal laminae/lenses/dust), so seams and seam_marks are empty. CONGLOMERATE at 33.25-34.50m has no dedicated class here and is recorded as sandstone with annotation. Depth markers 15.40, 18.20, 21.25, 27.00, 28.40, 30.95 are core-run/fracture-index boundaries, not lithological. '#' on intervals 2-3 = description based on Driller's log. Only handwriting on the sheets is the BGS reference 'NZ24SE/193' at each page top — no pencil seam-correlation marginalia. Remarks (sheet 4): inspection pit 1.50m dug by hand to clear services; chiselling 8.85-9.20m, 10.80-11.15m, 13.35-13.90m; water added 3.35-7.40m (may have masked ground-water); ground-water struck at 24.25m; full return of circulation air throughout rotary drilling; piezometer installed at 7.10m with backfill 39.30-7.90m bentonite/cement grout, 7.90-7.40m bentonite, 7.40-6.40m gravel, 6.40-5.90m bentonite, 5.90-0.50m bentonite/cement grout, 0.50m-GL concrete, stopcock cover concreted in.
